title: cassipourea sessiliflora
intro: Cassipourea sessiliflora, also known as the Sessile-flowered Cassipourea or Wild Pear, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a digestive aid and anti-diarrheal. Cassipourea sessiliflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including a decoction made from its roots and leaves, or a powder obtained from dried plant parts.
